sources.notes: Statement and constraints: count subsequences over values 1..3 satisfying the beautiful-sequence local-left/local-right conditions.
Official editorial solution keeps four DP counters and updates them while scanning the array.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu candidate source; not used in this review because the official editorial was sufficient.

annotation.summary: Count subsequences of length at least 3 where every non-first element has a smaller chosen element on the left and every non-last element has a larger chosen element on the right.
annotation.constraints: a_i is only 1, 2, or 3; total n over tests is at most 2e5, so a linear scan with modulo arithmetic is required.
annotation.core_idea: A beautiful subsequence over alphabet {1,2,3} must be exactly 1 followed by one or more 2s followed by 3. Maintain counts for empty prefix, chosen 1, chosen 1 plus some 2s, and completed 1-2*-3 subsequences; a value 2 can either extend an existing middle or start the middle after a 1.
annotation.complexity: O(n) per test case, O(1) extra memory.
tricks: Reduce the beauty conditions to the pattern 1, then at least one 2, then 3.
Scan left to right and update small DP counters by value.

solution_variants.name: value-state-counting-dp
solution_variants.summary: Use four counters: empty sequence, subsequences equal to 1, subsequences of form 1 followed by at least one 2, and completed 1-2*-3 subsequences. Update only the counter corresponding to the current value.

tags.evidence: The accepted solution is a counting DP over the prefix and the current pattern stage.
The quantity being computed is the number of subsequences satisfying a structural pattern.
